justification: All pass criteria met: (1) req.user?.id optional chaining risk identified — Kobe explains that undefined userId matches nullish customerId, bypassing auth. (2) Authorization before data access principle stated — fix explicitly says 'Add an early authentication guard before any DB call'. (3) IDOR order enumeration risk noted as a dedicated critical finding with 404/403 differentiation. (4) Critical severity assigned to the auth bypass, High to IDOR. (5) Verdict is BLOCK. (6) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the core undefined === undefined bypass — explained the mechanism clearly with reproduction steps
IDOR enumeration called out as a separate finding with a concrete fix (unify to 404)
Authorization-before-data-access principle explicitly stated in the fix
Bonus finding on input validation/injection risk adds value without diluting the critical findings
IDOR severity rated High rather than Critical — for a public-facing auth endpoint, order enumeration arguably warrants Critical per the rubric's guidance
Production readiness section is thorough, noting backward compatibility impact of status code changes on API consumers
